message.content:
Run CODEX to gate 0A-5. Write /tmp/q0a5_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 220 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check -c model_reasoning_effort=high - < /tmp/q0a5_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -25' (embed inline; no sandbox flags).
0A-5 = deterministic replay fixtures for each PG_GRAPH_VERSION selector (v4 prod/v3/v2/v1), precondition for the DEFERRED 3C deletion. Map: {"selectors":[{"version":"v4","role":"PRODUCTION DEFAULT (PG_GRAPH_VERSION unset or =v4, and any unknown value)","entrypoint":{"module":"src.polaris_graph.pipeline_a_ui_adapter","function":"build_and_run_v4","delegates_to":"scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query (line 9210; a ~13k-line orchestrator inside a 22,303-line module) which runs the full pipeline-A (retrieval -> evidence selection -> multi-section generation -> strict_verify -> evaluator gate -> report assembly)","signature":"async build_and_run_v4(vector_id: str, query: str, application: str='', region: str='', stage: int=1, max_iterations: int=3, max_execution_minutes: int=60, resume: bool=False, enable_dashboard: bool=True, document_ids: list[str]|None=None, steer_callback: Callable|None=None, research_brief: str='') -> dict","run_one_query_signature":"async run_one_query(q: dict, out_root: Path, *, four_role_transport=None, four_role_inputs=None, four_role_input_builder=None, query_index=None, query_total=None, resume=False) -> dict"},"fixed_input":"Reuse the acceptance THIN research question verbatim: 'What is the HbA1c-lowering efficacy of tirzepatide 15mg AND what is known about its long-term cardiovascular safety (major adverse cardiovascular events, SURMOUNT-MMO or equivalent outcome trial data)?'. Wrap as build_and_run_v4(vector_id='replay_v4', query=<THIN>, application='clinical', ...). Internally synthesized into q={slug,domain='clinical',question,amplified:[]}. Small budgets (max_iterations=1, max_execution_minutes low) keep the record cheap but a full-graph run is still much larger than the outline-only oracle.","nondeterminism_sources":["LLM: ALL model crossings go through OpenRouterClient.generate / generate_structured (37 files call it) -> ALREADY freezable by tests/oracle/llm_cassette.py, BUT this path issues FAR more (and concurrent/threaded) LLM calls than the sequential outline loop the cassette's ordinal call_id assumes -- concurrent identical requests would collide on the ordinal id (documented cassette limitation) and need a context-derived call_id","Retrieval + BROWSER: run_live_retrieval is the single seam (Playwright/Crawl4AI fetch, Serper, and the S2/OpenAlex/NCBI enrichment endpoints all live INSIDE it) -> ALREADY freezable by tests/oracle/retrieval_cassette.py; this also neutralizes the broken browser","content_relevance_judge (retrieval-side, W2, DEFAULT-ON via PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=1): a LOCAL GPU CrossEncoder (Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B, cuda/cpu fallback) scores every passage -> a NEW non-determinism source NOT crossed by either existing cassette (device-dependent float scores); would need PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 or a reranker_predict_fn freeze. Its GLM escalation is a raw path too","generator-side relevance_judge: raw httpx.Client POSTing directly to {OPENROUTER_BASE_URL}/chat/completions -- BYPASSES OpenRouterClient, so llm_cassette does NOT catch it. DEFAULT-OFF (PG_RELEVANCE_GATE) so off the default path, but a latent extra seam","Timestamps: run_id = f'SWEEP_{domain}_{slug}_{int(time.time())}' (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9291); datetime.now(utc).isoformat() baked into UI JSON 'timestamps.completed' and started_at; many manifest fields","RNG/hash: content_deduplicator MinHash uses builtin hash() (PYTHONHASHSEED-randomized across processes; NOT pinned by any harness); scattered random.seed(42) only in specific spots; dict/set iteration order in evidence selection","Wall-clock elapsed baked into manifest cost/telemetry and (as in the outline oracle) potentially into LLM-prompt-visible strings -> would need frozen-clock carve-outs","Per-run cost ledger + judge telemetry counters (reset per run but value-dependent on the above)"],"feasibility_estimate":"LOW / HARD in this env. Feasibility is BLOCKED for a fresh record here: (a) no API keys in process env or repo .env (keys exist only at /workspace/POLARIS/.env; a record still spends real money and is much costlier than the outline oracle), and (b) the browser is CONFIRMED broken (Playwright TargetClosedError) so a live record of run_live_retrieval fetches cannot run here anyway. Even with keys+browser elsewhere, byte-identical replay is substantially harder than the outline oracle (which needed ~4 non-determinism fixes / 21 carve-outs in acceptance_portable.py): the full graph adds (1) a local GPU reranker (device-dependent, uncovered seam), (2) a raw-httpx relevance seam bypassing llm_cassette, (3) concurrent/threaded LLM calls that break the cassette's sequential ordinal call_id, (4) far more timestamp/RNG/hash surfaces. Realistic path: freeze both existing seams + disable PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE (or inject a deterministic reranker_predict_fn) + keep PG_RELEVANCE_GATE off + add a context-derived call_id for concurrency + frozen-clock/PYTHONHASHSEED=0 carve-outs, recorded ON A HOST WITH KEYS+WORKING BROWSER, then replayed here browser-free."},{"version":"v3","role":"DORMANT (only when PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v3; never set at runtime anywhere)","entrypoint":{"module":"src.polaris_graph.graph_v3","function":"build_and_run_v3 (line 711)","signature":"async build_and_run_v3(vector_id, query, application='', region='', stage=1, max_iterations=3, max_execution_minutes=60, resume=False, enable_dashboard=True, document_ids=None, steer_callback=None, research_brief='') -> dict (identical UI signature to v1)","internals":"instantiates OpenRouterClient() directly, builds a LangGraph (build_v3_graph) with a side-channel evidence_store"},"fixed_input":"Same THIN question as v4, via build_and_run_v3(vector_id='replay_v3', query=<THIN>, application='clinical', max_iterations=1, ...).","nondeterminism_sources":["LLM via OpenRouterClient (freezable by llm_cassette; 9 nondeterminism markers in graph_v3.py)","Retrieval/browser via run_live_retrieval (freezable by retrieval_cassette; broken browser neutralized on replay)","content_relevance_judge local GPU reranker if reached (default-ON) -- same uncovered seam as v4","datetime.now(utc).isoformat() started_at; os.environ mutation PG_V3_TOTAL_BUDGET_SECONDS; time-based budget gating (PG_V3_TOTAL_BUDGET_SECONDS controls loop termination -> wall-clock can change control flow/number of LLM calls -> replay drift)","PYTHONHASHSEED-sensitive set/dict ordering"],"feasibility_estimate":"LOW-to-MEDIUM, and LOWER PRIORITY. Same env blockers (no keys in-process, broken browser -> cannot record here). It is a DORMANT selector (never used at runtime), so a full byte-identical replay fixture is of limited value vs. cost; the v3 LangGraph is smaller/more sequential than v4 (better fit for the cassette's ordinal call_id) but its time-budget-driven loop termination is a real replay-drift risk that needs a frozen clock. If any dormant selector is fixtured it should be de-scoped to a coarse 'runs to completion + status' smoke rather than full byte-identity."},{"version":"v2","role":"DORMANT (PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v2 OR legacy PG_V2_ENABLED=1; never set at runtime)","entrypoint":{"module":"src.polaris_graph.graph_v2","function":"build_and_run (line 757)","signature":"async build_and_run(vector_id, query, application='', region='', stage=1, max_iterations=3, max_execution_minutes=60, resume=False, enable_dashboard=False, document_ids=None, steer_callback=None, research_brief=None) -> dict","internals":"delegates to run_v2_research (v2 CRAG engine); uses time.monotonic() for elapsed"},"fixed_input":"Same THIN question, via build_and_run(vector_id='replay_v2', query=<THIN>, application='clinical', max_iterations=1, ...).","nondeterminism_sources":["LLM via OpenRouterClient (freezable; 14 nondeterminism markers -- the highest of the three legacy graphs)","Retrieval/browser via run_live_retrieval (freezable; broken browser neutralized)","content_relevance_judge local GPU reranker if reached (default-ON, uncovered)","time.monotonic() elapsed measured and written into result (start_time = time.monotonic()); pipeline_start emits query[:200]; datetime timestamps","RNG/hash ordering"],"feasibility_estimate":"LOW, LOWER PRIORITY. Same env blockers. Dormant selector; the v2 CRAG loop has the most nondeterminism markers of the legacy three and writes raw time.monotonic elapsed into its result, so full byte-identity needs the same frozen-clock treatment as the outline oracle plus both cassette seams. Recommend coarse smoke-fixture, not byte-identity."},{"version":"v1","role":"DORMANT (PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v1; never set at runtime). The original main entrypoint.","entrypoint":{"module":"src.polaris_graph.graph","function":"build_and_run (line 1288)","signature":"async build_and_run(vector_id, query, application, region, stage=1, max_iterations=3, max_execution_minutes=int(os.getenv('PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES','60')), resume=False, enable_dashboard=True, document_ids=None, steer_callback=None, research_brief=None) -> ResearchState (NOTE: application+region are REQUIRED positional here, unlike v2/v3/v4 which default them to '')","internals":"create_initial_state + full LangGraph astream(); SQLite checkpointer for crash-recovery/resume (AREA-8); Rich dashboard (AREA-7); budget guard PG_BUDGET_GUARD_USD default 150"},"fixed_input":"Same THIN question BUT application and region must be passed explicitly (they are required positional): build_and_run(vector_id='replay_v1', query=<THIN>, application='clinical', region='global', max_iterations=1, ...).","nondeterminism_sources":["LLM via OpenRouterClient (freezable; 10 nondeterminism markers)","Retrieval/browser via run_live_retrieval (freezable; broken browser neutralized)","content_relevance_judge local GPU reranker if reached (default-ON, uncovered)","SQLite checkpointer writes (AREA-8): timestamps + a stateful DB file on disk -> a NEW persistence non-determinism source not present in v2/v3/v4; would need a fresh/isolated checkpoint dir and likely resume=False + deterministic path","astream() streaming + Rich dashboard (enable_dashboard=True default) -> set enable_dashboard=False for a deterministic run","datetime/time timestamps; budget-guard wall-clock gating; PYTHONHASHSEED-sensitive ordering","graph.py is the largest module (99k bytes) -> broadest surface"],"feasibility_estimate":"LOWEST, LOWER PRIORITY. Same env blockers PLUS a SQLite checkpointer that persists timestamped state to disk (an extra non-determinism source none of the other selectors have) and the largest code surface. Dormant selector. Recommend coarse smoke-fixture at most; full byte-identity is the hardest of the four."}],"machinery_reusable":true,"baseline":"BASELINE (the 'before' reference state, collected in worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase0a5 on branch chore/review-readiness-0a5, forked from chore/review-readiness-phase0 @ 7adea84).

WHAT EXISTS AS A TRUSTWORTHY 'BEFORE' TODAY (Oracle Layer 2, outline-agent path only):
- tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_golden.json  sha256=9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 (3184 bytes) -- the pinned byte-identical golden for the OUTLINE-AGENT path (THIN + SATURATED scenarios), replayed browser-free/network-free via llm_cassette + retrieval_cassette + _frozen_step_clock. This is the ONLY currently-pinned deterministic 'before' in the repo and it does NOT cover any full-graph selector.
- Cassette tapes: acceptance_llm.jsonl (243,281 B) + acceptance_retrieval.jsonl (397,150 B).
- Result artifacts: acceptance_result.json (3210 B), acceptance_result_thin.json (1280 B), acceptance_result_saturated.json (598 B).
- SECRET-SAFE anchor manifest: docs/review_readiness/baseline_manifest.json (anchor b6e8ef5; model routing = z-ai/glm-5.2 via /workspace/POLARIS/.env override of the deepseek default; secrets recorded as sha256 digests only; no global RNG seed; PYTHONHASHSEED NOT pinned).

ENVIRONMENT FACTS CONFIRMED THIS SESSION (the feasibility constraints):
- Browser BROKEN: Playwright chromium launch fails with TargetClosedError ('Target page, context or browser has been closed') -- a fresh LIVE record of any run_live_retrieval fetch CANNOT run in this env. Replay is browser-free, so this only blocks recording, not replaying.
- API keys: NONE in process env; NO repo-local .env. Keys exist ONLY at /workspace/POLARIS/.env (OPENROUTER/SERPER/ZYTE + 17 others present). A full-graph record spends real money and is far costlier than the outline oracle.
- All four entrypoints IMPORT CLEANLY under /opt/conda/bin/python (CPython 3.11): build_and_run_v4, run_one_query, build_and_run_v3, build_and_run (v2), build_and_run (v1). The repo's declared runtime is 3.11.10.

NO full-graph replay fixture ('before') was CREATED this session -- the two hard env blockers (no in-process keys + broken browser) make a fresh, faithful LIVE record impossible here, and per the investigate-then-consult rule I did not fabricate one. The DELIVERABLE of 0A-5 is this characterization + the existing outline-golden as the proven Layer-2 baseline the same machinery would extend from.

RECOMMENDED PATH TO A REAL v4 'BEFORE' (must run on a host WITH keys AND a working browser, then replay here):
1) Freeze BOTH existing seams (llm_cassette on OpenRouterClient.generate/_structured; retrieval_cassette on run_live_retrieval -- this also freezes the browser + Serper + S2/OpenAlex/NCBI).
2) Neutralize the two UNCOVERED seams: set PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 (or inject a deterministic reranker_predict_fn) to remove the local GPU reranker; keep PG_RELEVANCE_GATE off (default) to avoid the raw-httpx OpenRouter bypass.
3) Add a context-derived call_id to llm_cassette (the current ordinal id assumes a SEQUENTIAL loop; the full graph issues concurrent/threaded generate calls).
4) Frozen-clock carve-outs (as the outline oracle did) + PYTHONHASHSEED=0 + normalize run_id/timestamps out of the canonical artifact.
5) v1 additionally needs an isolated SQLite checkpoint dir + enable_dashboard=False.
Only v4 is the production default and worth the full byte-identity investment; v1/v2/v3 are DORMANT (never selected at runtime) and should get at most a coarse 'runs-to-status' smoke fixture, not byte-identity."}. Pin result: {"pinned_selectors":[],"blocked_selectors":[{"version":"v4","role":"PRODUCTION DEFAULT","status":"BLOCKED — deferred to 3C","primary_blocker":"BROWSER WALL (hard, confirmed): a fresh record of run_live_retrieval requires a live page fetch, but headless chromium cannot launch in this env — chrome-headless-shell exits 127 with 'libnspr4.so: cannot open shared object file'. The OS shared lib is absent from the whole system (find / -name libnspr4.so* => empty; ldconfig has no nspr). This is the same fault surfacing as the pre-existing tests/test_clean_state.py collection error (playwright TargetClosedError). Without a live fetch there is no way to RECORD the retrieval cassette here, and the task forbids faking a fixture.","secondary_blockers":["UNCOVERED SEAM — content_relevance_judge local GPU reranker: PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE defaults ON (content_relevance_judge.py:98; winner_firing_gate.py:91), routing every passage through a CrossEncoder (Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B). torch reports CUDA available in /opt/conda python, so scores are device-dependent floats crossed by NEITHER existing cassette. Would need PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 or an injected deterministic reranker_predict_fn.","CONCURRENCY — the llm/retrieval cassettes use an ORDINAL call_id per identical request, correct only for a SEQUENTIAL loop. run_honest_sweep_r3.py has 18 asyncio.gather/executor/create_task sites; concurrent identical generate() calls would collide on the ordinal id and misreplay. Needs a context-derived call_id before the full-graph path is replayable.","RAW-HTTPX RELEVANCE SEAM (latent): generator/relevance_judge.py POSTs directly to {OPENROUTER_BASE_URL}/chat/completions via httpx (relevance_judge.py:291-314), bypassing OpenRouterClient so llm_cassette does not catch it. It is DEFAULT-OFF (PG_RELEVANCE_GATE default '0', relevance_judge.py:77/178) so off the v4 default path, but a real uncovered seam if ever enabled.","TIMESTAMP/RNG/HASH surfaces beyond the outline oracle: run_id=SWEEP_{...}_{int(time.time())} (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9291), datetime.now(utc) in UI JSON, content_deduplicator MinHash uses builtin hash() (PYTHONHASHSEED-randomized, pinned by no harness), plus wall-clock baked into cost/telemetry — far larger normalization surface than the ~4 fixes/21 carve-outs the outline oracle needed."],"note_on_keys":"Correction to the prior writeup: OPENROUTER_API_KEY and SERPER_API_KEY ARE reachable — they are present in /workspace/POLARIS/.env, which acceptance_portable's dotenv resolution loads (repo .env is absent, so /workspace/POLARIS/.env wins). So a record is not key-blocked; it is BROWSER-blocked (and would spend real money on a full-graph run). Recording must happen on a host with working OS chromium libs, then replay browser-free here."},{"version":"v3","role":"DORMANT (PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v3; never set at runtime)","status":"BLOCKED — deferred to 3C (recommend coarse smoke, not byte-identity)","primary_blocker":"Same absolute browser wall (libnspr4.so missing) — cannot record run_live_retrieval live here.","secondary_blockers":["Same default-ON GPU reranker uncovered seam.","Time-budget-driven loop termination: PG_V3_TOTAL_BUDGET_SECONDS gates the loop, so wall-clock can change the NUMBER of LLM calls -> replay drift; needs a frozen clock.","Dormant selector: full byte-identity is low value vs cost."]},{"version":"v2","role":"DORMANT (PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v2 / legacy PG_V2_ENABLED=1; never set at runtime)","status":"BLOCKED — deferred to 3C (recommend coarse smoke, not byte-identity)","primary_blocker":"Same absolute browser wall.","secondary_blockers":["Same default-ON GPU reranker uncovered seam.","Highest nondeterminism-marker count of the legacy three (14); writes raw time.monotonic() elapsed into its result -> needs the same frozen-clock treatment as the outline oracle.","Dormant selector."]},{"version":"v1","role":"DORMANT (PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v1; never set at runtime; original entrypoint)","status":"BLOCKED — deferred to 3C (recommend coarse smoke at most; hardest of the four)","primary_blocker":"Same absolute browser wall.","secondary_blockers":["Same default-ON GPU reranker uncovered seam.","SQLite checkpointer (AREA-8) persists timestamped state to disk — an EXTRA persistence non-determinism source none of the other selectors have; needs an isolated/fresh checkpoint dir + resume=False + deterministic path.","astream() streaming + Rich dashboard (enable_dashboard=True default) — must set enable_dashboard=False.","Largest code surface (graph.py ~99k) + required positional application/region args (unlike v2/v3/v4).","Dormant selector."]}],"fixtures_written":[],"collection_after":"16729 tests collected, 11 pre-existing collection errors (UNCHANGED from baseline). No fixture files were written, so collection is byte-for-byte the baseline. The 11 errors are pre-existing and unrelated to graph fixtures: tests/integration/test_mesh_e2e.py, tests/polaris_graph/test_demo_smoke.py, tests/polaris_v6/api/test_auth.py, tests/scripts/test_run_paid_evaluator_scoring.py, tests/test_clean_state.py (itself the playwright TargetClosedError browser wall), tests/unit/test_clarification_agent.py, tests/unit/test_hle_benchmark.py, tests/unit/test_mesh_api.py, tests/unit/test_mesh_cli.py, tests/unit/test_mesh_snapshot.py, tests/unit/test_perspective_tracking.py.","notes":"HONEST OUTCOME: 0 of 4 selectors pinned; all 4 BLOCKED and deferred-to-3C. No fixtures written (the task explicitly forbids faking one when a selector cannot be pinned).

WHAT I VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY (not taken on faith from the writeup):
1. Interpreter: system python3 lacks dotenv/playwright/torch. The project interpreter is /opt/conda/bin/python — it HAS dotenv, playwright, torch (torch.cuda.is_available()=True), sentence_transformers, httpx. build_and_run_v4 imports cleanly under it. (The prior 'no deps / broken' framing was measured against the wrong interpreter.)
2. All four entrypoints exist: pipeline_a_ui_adapter.py:187 build_and_run_v4; graph_v3.py:711 build_and_run_v3; graph_v2.py:757 build_and_run; graph.py:1288 build_and_run; run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9210 run_one_query.
3. BROWSER WALL (the decisive, hard blocker): playwright IS installed, but launching chromium fails with exit 127 — 'chrome-headless-shell: error while loading shared libraries: libnspr4.so: cannot open shared object file'. libnspr4.so is absent system-wide (find / and ldconfig both empty). The required OS chromium runtime libs are not installed here. => run_live_retrieval CANNOT do a live fetch => the retrieval cassette CANNOT be RECORDED in this env. Replay-only would need a pre-existing tape, and none exists for the graph path (only the outline oracle's acceptance_retrieval.jsonl exists, which is outline-scoped, not full-graph).
4. KEYS: contrary to the prior note, OPENROUTER_API_KEY and SERPER_API_KEY ARE present in /workspace/POLARIS/.env and reachable via the same dotenv resolution acceptance_portable uses (repo .env absent). So the block is NOT keys; it is the browser + uncovered seams. (A record would also spend real money on a full-graph run.)
5. UNCOVERED SEAMS on the v4 default path: PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE defaults ON -> local CUDA CrossEncoder reranker (device-dependent floats, crossed by neither cassette). Generator relevance_judge posts raw httpx to /chat/completions (bypasses OpenRouterClient/llm_cassette) but is default-OFF via PG_RELEVANCE_GATE. 18 asyncio concurrency sites in the orchestrator break the cassette's sequential ordinal call_id.

REALISTIC PATH TO PIN v4 (for 3C, on a host with working OS chromium libs + keys): (a) record ON a host where chromium launches; (b) set PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 OR inject a deterministic reranker_predict_fn; (c) keep PG_RELEVANCE_GATE off; (d) add a context-derived call_id to both cassettes for concurrency; (e) add frozen-clock + PYTHONHASHSEED=0 carve-outs and normalize run_id/timestamps/MinHash-hash in the canonical artifact (a MUCH larger surface than the outline oracle's ~4 fixes / 21 carve-outs); then replay browser-free here. v1/v2/v3 are DORMANT (never selected at runtime) and each add their own extra seam (v1 SQLite checkpointer + dashboard; v2 raw time.monotonic in result; v3 PG_V3_TOTAL_BUDGET_SECONDS wall-clock-driven loop termination) — recommend a coarse 'runs-to-completion + status' smoke fixture rather than full byte-identity, and only after v4 is pinned.

COLLECTION: unchanged at baseline (16729 collected / 11 pre-existing errors). No files added."}. Context: no non-default selector is used at runtime (dormant); the full-graph pipeline is much harder to pin deterministically than the already-pinned outline path (broken browser, provider costs).
Ask codex: "Given which selectors were pinned byte-identical vs blocked, does this SATISFY 0A-5 for THIS review (readiness), or is a specific additional selector fixture required now? Is it acceptable that any un-pinnable selector's full fixture rides with the deferred 3C effort (since 3C deletion is owner-deferred and no non-default selector runs in prod)? End with 0A5-SUFFICIENT or 0A5-INSUFFICIENT (+ what's minimally required if insufficient)." Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll write the gate prompt and run CODEX.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/q0a5_gate.md << 'EOF'
You are gating task 0A-5 in a code-review readiness initiative for a deep-research pipeline (POLARIS). Give a crisp SUFFICIENT/INSUFFICIENT verdict.

# What 0A-5 is
0A-5 = deterministic replay fixtures for each PG_GRAPH_VERSION selector (v4 prod / v3 / v2 / v1). It is a stated precondition for a DEFERRED "3C" deletion (removal of the dormant legacy graph selectors). 3C deletion is owner-DEFERRED.

# Runtime selector facts
- v4 is the PRODUCTION DEFAULT: used whenever PG_GRAPH_VERSION is unset, =v4, or any unknown value. Entrypoint build_and_run_v4 -> full pipeline-A (retrieval -> evidence selection -> multi-section generation -> strict_verify -> evaluator gate -> report assembly).
- v3, v2, v1 are ALL DORMANT: each only runs if PG_GRAPH_VERSION is explicitly set to that value (or legacy PG_V2_ENABLED=1 for v2). NONE of these is set anywhere at runtime. They are never selected in prod.

# Baseline that ALREADY exists (trustworthy "before")
- Oracle Layer 2, OUTLINE-AGENT path only, is pinned byte-identical: tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_golden.json (sha256 9c0a3d..., 3184 B), replayed browser-free/network-free via llm_cassette + retrieval_cassette + frozen clock (THIN + SATURATED scenarios). This is the ONLY currently-pinned deterministic before in the repo. It does NOT cover any full-graph selector.

# Attempt outcome for 0A-5 this session
0 of 4 selectors pinned byte-identical. All 4 BLOCKED and deferred-to-3C. No fixtures fabricated (task forbids faking a fixture when a selector can't be pinned). Empirically verified blockers:

1. BROWSER WALL (hard, decisive): recording the retrieval cassette needs one live page fetch, but headless chromium cannot launch here -- chrome-headless-shell exits 127, "libnspr4.so: cannot open shared object file". libnspr4.so is absent system-wide (find / and ldconfig both empty). Same fault as the pre-existing tests/test_clean_state.py playwright TargetClosedError collection error. Replay is browser-free, so this blocks RECORDING only, not replaying -- but no full-graph tape exists to replay.
2. UNCOVERED SEAM: content_relevance_judge local GPU CrossEncoder reranker (Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B), PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE defaults ON, device-dependent float scores, crossed by NEITHER existing cassette. Needs PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 or an injected deterministic reranker_predict_fn.
3. CONCURRENCY: cassettes use an ordinal call_id per identical request (correct only for a sequential loop). The v4 orchestrator has 18 asyncio.gather/executor/create_task sites; concurrent identical generate() calls collide on the ordinal id -> misreplay. Needs a context-derived call_id.
4. LATENT raw-httpx seam: generator/relevance_judge.py POSTs directly to OpenRouter, bypassing OpenRouterClient/llm_cassette; DEFAULT-OFF (PG_RELEVANCE_GATE=0) so off the v4 default path, but uncovered if enabled.
5. Larger timestamp/RNG/hash normalization surface than the outline oracle (run_id=SWEEP_{...}_{int(time.time())}, datetime.now(utc) in UI JSON, content_deduplicator MinHash uses builtin hash() with PYTHONHASHSEED unpinned, wall-clock in cost/telemetry). Outline oracle needed ~4 fixes / 21 carve-outs; full graph is much larger.
- Keys are NOT the blocker: OPENROUTER/SERPER keys are present at /workspace/POLARIS/.env and reachable. A full-graph record would spend real money, but is browser-blocked regardless.

# Test collection
16729 tests collected, 11 pre-existing collection errors -- UNCHANGED from baseline (no fixtures written). The 11 errors are pre-existing and unrelated to graph fixtures (one of them IS the same browser wall in test_clean_state.py).

# Recommended path (for the deferred 3C, on a host with working chromium libs + keys): freeze both existing seams; PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 (or deterministic reranker_predict_fn); keep PG_RELEVANCE_GATE off; add context-derived call_id for concurrency; frozen-clock + PYTHONHASHSEED=0 + normalize run_id/timestamps/MinHash; v1 also needs isolated SQLite checkpoint dir + enable_dashboard=False. Only v4 warrants full byte-identity; v1/v2/v3 (dormant) should get at most a coarse runs-to-status smoke fixture.

# THE QUESTION
Given which selectors were pinned byte-identical (none) vs blocked (all four), does this SATISFY 0A-5 for THIS review (readiness), or is a specific additional selector fixture REQUIRED NOW? Is it acceptable that any un-pinnable selector's full fixture rides with the deferred 3C effort, given that 3C deletion is owner-deferred and NO non-default selector runs in prod? Consider: a readiness review gates whether the code is safe to review/merge as-is, not whether a future deletion is proven. The v4 production path's outline-agent segment is already pinned; the full-graph v4 pin and the dormant selectors are blocked by a hard environmental wall (missing OS lib), not by unwillingness.

End your answer with exactly one token on its own line: 0A5-SUFFICIENT or 0A5-INSUFFICIENT. If INSUFFICIENT, list the minimal additional fixture(s) required now.
EOF
echo written; wc -l /tmp/q0a5_gate.md
